title: COMPUTEX 2026: S2C and Andes Technology Showcase Hardcore "EDA+IP" Synergy for the AI Era - Semiwiki
gist: At COMPUTEX 2026, FPGA prototyping vendor S2C and RISC-V IP provider Andes Technology demonstrated live LLM inference on S2C's AMD Versal-based Prodigy S8-100 system using Andes' AX66 RVA23-compliant processor, positioning their integrated EDA+IP workflow as a solution to soaring AI SoC complexity. The collaboration showcases a 'shift-left' verification methodology that enables hardware and software co-development in parallel, reducing time-to-market for AI and HPC chips. The demo highlights RISC-V's viability for edge AI applications, with chips now reaching hundreds of billions of gates requiring multi-FPGA prototyping environments.

themes.theme: AI SoC Complexity Driving Demand for Integrated EDA+IP Prototyping

insights.claim: Modern AI and HPC SoC designs are approaching hundreds of billions of gates, rendering traditional single-FPGA verification and prototyping methodologies insufficient.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Growing chip complexity is structurally expanding the addressable market for advanced FPGA-based prototyping solutions like S2C's Prodigy platform.
insights.quote: chip sizes are approaching hundreds of billions of gates, creating significant challenges for traditional verification and prototyping methodologies

insights.claim: The surge of AI and HPC workloads is forcing SoCs to incorporate multi-core processor clusters, specialized AI accelerators, and custom instruction extensions simultaneously, multiplying verification complexity.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: This multi-component integration trend underpins demand for complete hardware-software co-design environments that can validate the entire stack before silicon.
insights.quote: Modern designs routinely incorporate multi-core processor clusters, specialized AI accelerators, and custom instruction extensions.

insights.claim: S2C positions itself as the world's largest company focused exclusively on prototyping verification solutions, a narrow specialization that becomes increasingly valuable as SoC gate counts rise.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Pure-play specialization in prototyping may command premium pricing and customer stickiness as no single competitor offers an equivalent dedicated platform breadth.
insights.quote: S2C, the world's largest company focused exclusively on prototyping verification solutions

themes.theme: RISC-V Edge AI Viability Demonstrated with Live LLM Inference

insights.claim: S2C and Andes Technology ran two concurrent virtual machines each executing a Llama2-based LLM on a RISC-V AX66 processor under Linux KVM, demonstrating RISC-V's capability for multi-instance AI inference at the edge.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Live multi-VM LLM inference on RISC-V hardware reduces skepticism about RISC-V's AI readiness and signals growing ecosystem maturity for edge AI deployments.
insights.quote: the system launched two virtual machines, each executing a Large Language Model (LLM) based on the Llama2 architecture

insights.claim: The TinyStories model (~42 million parameters) running on Andes' AX66 RISC-V processor demonstrates a practical edge AI deployment where power consumption, cost, and deployment flexibility outweigh absolute model size.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Edge AI inference on RISC-V is a credible market opportunity for sub-frontier model workloads, particularly in cost-sensitive or power-constrained deployments.
insights.quote: TinyStories demonstrated how RISC-V processors can efficiently support edge AI applications where power consumption, cost, and deployment flexibility are critical considerations.

insights.claim: Andes Technology's AX66 processor IP is compliant with the RVA23 RISC-V standard, signaling alignment with the latest RISC-V international specification that broadens software ecosystem compatibility.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: RVA23 compliance positions Andes IP as compatible with an expanding RISC-V software stack, reducing adoption friction for chip designers selecting processor IP.
insights.quote: Andes' AX66 processor IP, compliant with the latest RVA23 RISC-V standard

themes.theme: AMD Versal as Preferred Silicon for High-Capacity FPGA Prototyping

insights.claim: S2C's Prodigy S8-100 prototyping system is built on AMD's Versal Premium VP1902 adaptive SoC, delivering approximately 100 million ASIC-gate equivalent capacity per FPGA — a benchmark for validating large-scale multi-core and AI-intensive designs.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: AMD's Versal platform is being adopted in advanced commercial prototyping systems, reinforcing AMD's position in the high-end FPGA market beyond traditional compute applications.
insights.quote: S2C's latest-generation Prodigy S8-100 prototyping system, powered by the AMD Versal™ Premium VP1902 adaptive SoC

insights.claim: Single-FPGA solutions are structurally insufficient for validating today's largest AI SoC designs, driving a shift to multi-FPGA prototyping architectures as the industry standard for advanced verification.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Multi-FPGA platform vendors with strong partitioning and debugging toolchains gain competitive moats as design complexity outpaces single-FPGA capacity limits.
insights.quote: As semiconductor designs continue to grow in complexity, single-FPGA solutions often struggle to provide sufficient capacity.

themes.theme: Shift-Left Verification Methodology for AI Chip Development

insights.claim: S2C's FPGA-based prototyping ecosystem enables hardware and software development to proceed in parallel, allowing validation of operating systems, drivers, middleware, and AI software stacks concurrently with hardware verification.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Parallel hardware-software co-development compresses AI chip development timelines, a key competitive differentiator for fabless companies racing to market.
insights.quote: By allowing hardware and software development to proceed in parallel, companies can significantly reduce development risk and accelerate time-to-market.

insights.claim: S2C's Player Pro tool automates compilation, design partitioning, and multi-FPGA debugging, lowering the engineering burden of operating large-scale FPGA prototyping environments.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Toolchain automation reduces the specialized skill requirement for prototyping infrastructure, broadening the addressable customer base for S2C's platform.
insights.quote: tools such as Player Pro, which simplify compilation, design partitioning, and multi-FPGA debugging

insights.claim: S2C's strategy centers on enabling early architecture evaluation and software validation before silicon tape-out, which the company frames as essential for reducing development risk on AI and RISC-V designs.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Early pre-silicon software validation reduces costly re-spins and shortens time-to-revenue, making robust prototyping infrastructure a strategic capex for leading fabless companies.
insights.quote: enable engineers to evaluate architectures, validate software, and identify hardware issues much earlier in the design cycle

themes.theme: Open Ecosystem Strategy in Semiconductor IP and EDA

insights.claim: S2C's VP explicitly stated that at hundreds-of-billions-of-gate design complexity, 'no single company can innovate alone,' signaling that open ecosystem partnerships between EDA, IP, and foundry partners are now a structural necessity.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: EDA and IP companies that build deep interoperability partnerships will be better positioned than isolated point-solution vendors as AI chip complexity scales.
insights.quote: When designs reach hundreds of billions of gates, no single company can innovate alone.

insights.claim: S2C is building a RISC-V open ecosystem by integrating processor IP, architecture evaluation, software development, and application validation into a unified pre-silicon workflow, targeting the full design-to-production lifecycle.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Ecosystem lock-in via integrated workflows is a durable competitive advantage — companies that win the pre-silicon workflow battle may retain customers through the entire product generation.
insights.quote: S2C is focused on lowering the barriers to innovation by building an open ecosystem that integrates processor IP, architecture evaluation, software development, and application validation early in the design process.

insights.claim: S2C's global support infrastructure provides localized technical assistance worldwide, a go-to-market differentiator for customers in Asia, Europe, and North America who require hands-on prototyping support.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Localized technical support reduces customer switching costs in the prototyping market, where engineer familiarity with toolchains creates stickiness.
insights.quote: a global support infrastructure that provides localized technical assistance worldwide

themes.theme: COMPUTEX 2026 as AI and HPC Industry Showcase

insights.claim: COMPUTEX 2026 operated under the theme 'AI Together,' with AI accelerators and advanced computing platforms dominating the exhibition floor, reflecting the industry's near-total pivot to AI-centric silicon.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The exhibition-floor dominance of AI accelerator companies underscores that infrastructure AI spend remains robust heading into the second half of 2026, with no visible slowdown.
insights.quote: AI accelerators and advanced computing platforms dominated the exhibition floor
